steps.observed: isAllowedHostname uses new URL(urlString).hostname with allowedDomains.some(domain => hostname === domain || hostname.endsWith('.' + domain))

steps.expected: Calls isAllowedHostname with youtube.com and google.com

steps.action: Verify no url.includes() domain checks in main.js
steps.expected: Zero matches for includes with youtube.com or google.com
Allows youtube.com/google.com domains, blocks includes-bypass attempts
steps.observed: 9/9 tests passed: allows youtube.com, www.youtube.com, music.youtube.com, google.com, www.google.com; blocks evil.com?ref=youtube.com, youtube.com.evil.com, notgoogle.com, invalid URLs

evidence.functionUsesUrlParsing: isAllowedHostname at line 10 uses new URL(urlString).hostname
evidence.noIncludesChecks: No url.includes() domain checks found in main.js

steps.expected: Calls isAllowedHostname with accounts.google.com and google.com
Allows accounts.google.com and *.google.com, blocks includes-bypass attempts
steps.observed: 8/8 tests passed: allows accounts.google.com, google.com, www.google.com, sub.google.com; blocks evil.com?ref=accounts.google.com, accounts.google.com.evil.com, notgoogle.com, evil.com

summary: Tested 2 assertions: 2 passed. VAL-CORS-002 confirmed will-navigate uses proper hostname-based URL parsing (isAllowedHostname with new URL().hostname) instead of url.includes(). VAL-CORS-003 confirmed setWindowOpenHandler uses the same hostname-based approach for accounts.google.com and google.com.
